content: The language education definition of reflection as a deliberate vocabulary learning metacognitive step can be borrowed to refine LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as learners strengthen vocabulary retention by explicitly stopping to reflect on the meaning and usage of new terms after encountering them, large language models can benefit from scheduled, discrete reflection pauses after completing task output, rather than running reflection as a continuous concurrent process. This structured reflexion routine can reduce computational overhead while improving the consistency of self-correction.
